Journal.Title: The international journal of medical robotics + computer assisted surgery : MRCAS

Article.ArticleTitle: Controlling of Applied Force and Cornea Displacement Estimation in Robotic Corneal Surgery With a Gripper Surgical Instrument.

Abstract.AbstractText: The human eye consists of highly sensitive, hydrated, and relatively thin tissues, making precise control and accurate force estimation crucial in robotic eye surgery. This paper introduces a novel control method and state observer designed for a gripper surgical instrument used on the external ocular surface during robotic eye surgery.

A novel state observer, operating in tandem with the controller, estimates the applied force. The proposed control approach, termed the Fixed-time Observer-based Sliding Mode Control (FOSMC), estimates the applied force by determining the gripper states and uses an eye model to calculate its displacement.

The performance of the proposed control method was compared with two other finite-time and asymptotic techniques across two scenarios. The results demonstrated excellent performance using the proposed method.
The FOSMC control technique effectively estimates the applied force during robotic eye surgery, making it a reliable solution for controlling the gripper surgical instrument.

AffiliationInfo.Affiliation: Institute of Electronic Systems, Faculty of Electronics and Information Technology, Warsaw University of Technology, Warsaw, Poland.
The Institute of Automatic Control and Robotics, Faculty of Mechatronics, Warsaw University of Technology, Warsaw, Poland.
Faculty of Health, Education, Medicine and Social Care, Medical Technology Research Centre, The Institute of Excellence in Robotic Surgery, Anglia Ruskin University, Chelmsford, UK.

MedlineCitation.CoiStatement: The authors declare no conflicts of interest.
